title: Papaver racemosum
intro: Papaver racemosum, also known as the racemed poppy or California poppy, is a perennial plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including relieving anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its sedative and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Papaver racemosum include alkaloids such as meconic acid and protopine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Papaver racemosum is a species of poppy in the family Papaveraceae. It belongs to the genus Papaver, which is a group of flowering plants. The Papaver racemosum is further classified into the order Ranunculales, which is a large order of flowering plants. The family Papaveraceae is a group of flowering plants that includes the poppies, the meadowfoam, and the bloodroot. Papaver racemosum is native to the mountainous regions of western North America.
